steps.title: Identify the key comparison
steps.content: The passage directly states: “they [tax havens] are rarely used by investors living in Japan, while many investors who utilize tax havens reside in Saudi Arabia or Greece.” This contrasts Japan as the low-usage outlier against Saudi Arabia's high usage.

steps.title: Apply the comparison
steps.content: B captures this relationship exactly: Japan investors are less likely than Saudi Arabia investors to store wealth in Singapore, which is explicitly named as a tax haven. The other three either contradict the stated usage patterns (A claims Japan investors engage in tax haven use; D claims Japan uses them more than Greece) or introduce investment analysis the passage never addresses (C). That matches choice B — B is correct.
